name: Rewrite active PTX hot-band B delivery at the consumer boundary without CTA repack

summary.name: Rewrite active PTX hot-band B delivery at the consumer boundary without CTA repack
summary.hypothesis: The restored `26.093568 ms` PTX hot-band branch proves that the active `128x128 K16` surface is now the right place to keep pushing, but the branch is still far from the `20 ms` target: tensor-active is only `48.43%`, barrier is `8.05%`, and `mio_throttle` remains `4.45%` while DRAM (`30.93%`) and L2 (`25.77%`) are not saturated. The best next move is to keep the existing 16-byte `cp.async` producer path and double-buffered shared tiles intact, then rewrite only the shared-to-register B consumer path inside the active PTX microkernel: warp-local permuted or XOR swizzle, explicit `ldmatrix` or lane-remap delivery, and tighter mirrored fragment reuse so operand delivery happens at the warp boundary instead of through any new CTA-level repack. This directly accepts the human-idea families `Bank Conflict`, `Register Reuse`, and `Ps2r` on the real active path.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Shared-memory B-fragment delivery and consumer-side feed behavior on the active PTX hot band, especially `mio_throttle`, short-scoreboard, and bank friction that keep Tensor Cores underfed.
summary.risk: Medium-high risk: the change is still bounded to the active PTX branch, but a consumer-only B rewrite can silently break fragment ownership, increase register pressure, or worsen bank behavior if the new lane mapping stops matching `ptx_wmma_load_b_row` and the mirrored MMA order.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if one bounded iteration fails to improve tensor-active or consumer-feed stalls materially versus the `26.093568 ms` branch, or if bank/occupancy pressure rises enough to erase the gain.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
